title: Trisetum wrangelense
intro: Trisetum wrangelense, also known as Wrangel's hair grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and Russia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Trisetum wrangelense include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Trisetum wrangelense is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically in the form of a poultice or salve, to take advantage of its health benefits.
